Greek-Entry.English-Entry: filament appended to the lips of certain fishes
have the fasces borne before one
the insignia of the lictor, fasces
a light matter, you think little
recklessly, do wrong, play the rogue
live a lazy life, take things easily
give one a slap on the buttocks
easiness of temper, taking things easily
find relief from pain, recover from illness
to be broken down, crushed by suffering
who tricked out his heroes in rags
the inner corner of the eye
a venomous kind of spider, malmignatte
τὴν καλάμην, καὶ τοὺς ἐν αὐτῇ αὐλοῦντας ῥαπαύλους
τὸν ποιητήν, Δωριεῖς, ἢ ποικιλτήν, ἢ τὰς κρηπῖδας ποιοῦντα
strike with a stick, cudgel, thrash
easiness of temper, good nature, mildness
resting-time, season of calm and tranquillity
thrust a radish up the fundament
the lower part of the back, the chine
the sharp projection on the middle of the shoulder-blade
he who cuts the victim through the spine
portion of an Epic poem fit for recitation at one time
bard who recited his own poem
that which flows, a river, stream
to be unsteady, act at random
incline one way or the other
in a state of flux, flowing
to be in a tumble-down condition
to be in perpetual flux and change
one who has such a rupture
sea breaking on the beach, surf
having let loose, having burst into
that breaks the earth into fissures
might to break through armed ranks
to be a public speaker, practise oratory
skilled in speaking, fit to be an orator
a compact between the Law-giver and the People
one who threw convicts into the sea
at whose name one shudders, horrible
shudder to do, shrink from doing
hot bath administerea' to an epileptic patient
that from which anything springs as from a root
of which the flower grows straight from the root
with a stripc of vegetable purple
cut up by the roots, extirpate
for the cutting and gathering of roots
ῥοιαὶ μεγάλαι. ἄμεινον δὲ διὰ τοῦ ξ ξίμβαι
ἀγκύλη τοῦ ὤμου, οἱ δὲ τὸν βραχίονα τοῦ ἱερείου
with a nose reaching to the chin, with a nutcracker nose and chin
tracking by the nose, hunting by scent
that which is filed off, filings
instrument for taking hold of the nose
force with which anything is thrown
throw to and fro, toss about
make a bold throw, run a risk
throwing away one's shield in battle, craven
ταχυτὴς περὶ τὸ ἰδεῖν τὸ ποθούμενον
a kind of cup made at Rhodes
to have one's grave decked with roses
ceremony at which graves were decked with roses,
a confection of roses and quinces
extract of roses prepared with honey
streams of events, tide of affairs
waves dashing on the beach, breakers, waves
the dash and sound of oars
rushing noise, roar of waves, dash of oars
one who moves with a rushing sound
bring into the shape of a rhombus
made in the shape of a rhombus, panelled in lozenges
τὰ ἐκ τῆς συκαμίνου μόρα τὰ ἄωρα ξηρανθέντα
in which each word is one syllable longer than that before
turn of the scale, fall of the scale-pan, weight
small additional weight, make-weight, casting weight,
that which is supped up, thick gruel
with a strong stream, running violently
in which there are strong currents
running from the eyes without external cause
that go in shoals with the currents
a disease of the eye causing a continual weeping discharge
bring into a measure of time
to make ducks and drakes' of money
anything used for washing, soap, lye
stolen property taken back as compensation for the theft
in search of persons to seize and hold to ransom
strap by which one holds a horse, rein
with loose rein, at full gallop
boy of less than one year old
πληγαὶ ὕφαιμοι διακεκομμέναι, οἱ δὲ μώλωπες
ῥώμην, ἰσχύν, ὅρμημα, ὡς γνῶμα γνώμην
hold with Rome, be of the Roman party
ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ἔχητε πλοῖον, συνεμβήσητε ἅμα ἡμῖν εἰς ῥώμσιν
